169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p, do_top_a) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	        self._do_top_a = do_top_a
225	
226	    def forward(
227	        self,
228	        logits: torch.Tensor,
229	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
230	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
231	        """
232	        Single-step scheduling:
233	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
234	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
235	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
236	
237	        Multi-step scheduling:
238	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
239	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
240	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
241	            tensor
242	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
243	            in the
244	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
245	            structure
246	
247	        Args:
248	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
249	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
250	        """
251	        assert logits is not None
252	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
253	
254	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
255	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
256	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
257	        elif self._do_penalties:
258	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
259	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
260	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
261	            # between decode runs.
262	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
263	
264	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
265	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
266	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
267	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
268	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
269	        do_top_a = self._do_top_a
270	
271	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
272	
273	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
274	        if do_penalties:
275	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
278	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
279	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
280	
281	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
282	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
283	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
284	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
285	
286	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
287	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
288	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
289	
290	        if do_min_p:
291	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
292	
293	        if do_top_a:
294	            logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
295	
296	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
297	        # Compute the probabilities.
298	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
299	        # Compute the log probabilities.

100	        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
101	        self.num_prompt_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
102	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
103	            (max_num_reqs, ),
104	            device="cpu",
105	            dtype=torch.int32,
106	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
107	        )
108	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
109	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()
110	
111	        # Block table.
112	        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
113	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
114	            max_model_len=max_model_len,
115	            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
116	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
117	            device=device,
118	            block_sizes=block_sizes,
119	        )
120	
121	        # Sampling-related.
122	        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
123	                                       dtype=torch.float32,
124	                                       device=device)
125	        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
126	                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
127	                                                  device="cpu",
128	                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
129	        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
130	        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
131	        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()
132	
133	        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
134	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
135	                                 device=device)
136	        self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
137	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
138	                                            device="cpu",
139	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
140	        self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
141	        self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
142	
143	        self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
144	                                 dtype=torch.int32,
145	                                 device=device)
146	        self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
147	                                            dtype=torch.int32,
148	                                            device="cpu",
149	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
150	        self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()
151	        self.top_k_reqs: set[str] = set()
152	
153	        self.top_a = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
154	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
155	                                 device=device)
156	        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
157	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
158	                                            device="cpu",
159	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
160	        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
161	        self.top_a_reqs: set[str] = set()
162	
163	        # IDs of requests which do not support spec decoding
164	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs: set[str] = set()
165	
166	        # Frequency penalty related data structures
167	        self.frequency_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
168	                                               dtype=torch.float,
169	                                               device=device)
170	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
171	            (max_num_reqs, ),
172	            dtype=torch.float,
173	            device="cpu",
174	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
175	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu = \
176	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
177	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
178	
179	        # Presence penalty related data structures
180	        self.presence_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
181	                                              dtype=torch.float,
182	                                              device=device)
183	        self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
184	                                                         dtype=torch.float,
185	                                                         device="cpu",
186	                                                         pin_memory=pin_memory)
187	        self.presence_penalties_cpu = self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy(
188	        )
189	        self.presence_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
190	
191	        # Repetition penalty related data structures
192	        self.repetition_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
193	                                                dtype=torch.float,
194	                                                device=device)
195	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
196	            (max_num_reqs, ),
197	            dtype=torch.float,
198	            device="cpu",
199	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
200	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu = \
201	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
202	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
203	
204	        # lora related
205	        self.request_lora_mapping = np.zeros((self.max_num_reqs, ),
206	                                             dtype=np.int32)
207	        self.lora_id_to_request_ids: dict[int, set[str]] = {}
208	        self.lora_id_to_lora_request: dict[int, LoRARequest] = {}
209	
210	        # req_index -> generator
211	        # NOTE(woosuk): The indices of the requests that do not have their own
212	        # generator should not be included in the dictionary.
213	        self.generators: dict[int, torch.Generator] = {}
214	
215	        self.num_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
216	        # NOTE(rob): num_prompt_logprobs only includes reqs
217	        # that are currently in the prefill phase.
218	        self.num_prompt_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
219	
220	        # To accumulate prompt logprobs tensor chunks across prefill steps.
221	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu: dict[str, LogprobsTensors] = {}
222	
223	        # Internal representation of per-step batch state changes, used for
224	        # reordering persistent batch and generating logitsprocs batch state
225	        # updates. Should reset each step.
226	        self.batch_update_builder = BatchUpdateBuilder()
227	
228	        # Define logits processors.
229	        # TODO(andy): logits processor list should be extensible via engine
230	        # constructor argument; for now the list is fixed.
231	        self.logitsprocs = init_builtin_logitsprocs(
232	            pin_memory_available=pin_memory,
233	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1,
234	            device=device)
235	
236	        # TODO convert this to LogitsProcessor
237	        self.has_allowed_token_ids: set[str] = set()
238	        # NOTE(lufang): In the mask tensor, if the corresponding token allowed,
239	        # the value is False. Since we use masked_fill_ to set -inf.
240	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
241	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
242	
243	        # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
244	        self.bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]] = {}
245	
246	        self.req_output_token_ids: list[Optional[list[int]]] = []
247	
248	        # This is updated each time the batch constituents change.
249	        self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()
250	
251	        self.pooling_params: dict[str, PoolingParams] = {}
252	
253	    @property
254	    def req_ids(self) -> list[str]:
255	        # None elements should only be present transiently
256	        # while performing state updates to the batch.
257	        return cast(list[str], self._req_ids)
258	
259	    def _get_next_add_index(self) -> int:
260	        if (req_index := self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()) is not None:
261	            # Fill the empty index.
262	            return req_index
263	        # Append to end
264	        return self.num_reqs

266	    def _register_add_request(self, request: "CachedRequestState") -> int:
267	        """Track add-request operations"""
268	        req_index = self._get_next_add_index()
269	        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
270	        params = (request.sampling_params
271	                  if request.sampling_params else request.pooling_params)
272	        self.batch_update_builder.added.append(
273	            (req_index, params, request.output_token_ids))
274	        return req_index

276	    def add_request(
277	        self,
278	        request: "CachedRequestState",
279	    ) -> int:
280	        req_index = self._register_add_request(request)
281	
282	        req_id = request.req_id
283	        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
284	            self._req_ids.append(req_id)
285	            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
286	        else:
287	            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
288	            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids
289	
290	        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index
291	
292	        # Copy the prompt token ids and output token ids.
293	        num_prompt_tokens = len(request.prompt_token_ids)
294	        self.num_prompt_tokens[req_index] = num_prompt_tokens
295	        self.token_ids_cpu[
296	            req_index, :num_prompt_tokens] = request.prompt_token_ids
297	        start_idx = num_prompt_tokens
298	        end_idx = start_idx + len(request.output_token_ids)
299	        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,

300	                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
301	        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
302	        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
303	        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
304	        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
305	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens
306	
307	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
308	        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)
309	
310	        if sampling_params := request.sampling_params:
311	            if (self.is_spec_decode
312	                    and is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params)):
313	                self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.add(req_id)
314	            if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
315	                # Avoid later division by zero.
316	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
317	                self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
318	            else:
319	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
320	                self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
321	
322	            self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
323	            if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
324	                self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
325	            top_k = sampling_params.top_k
326	            if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
327	                self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
328	            else:
329	                top_k = self.vocab_size
330	            self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
331	            self.top_a_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_a
332	            if sampling_params.top_a > 0.0:
333	                self.top_a_reqs.add(req_id)
334	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
335	                req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
336	            if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
337	                self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
338	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
339	                req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
340	            if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
341	                self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
342	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
343	                req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
344	            if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
345	                self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
346	
347	            # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
348	            # do not have their own generator.
349	            if request.generator is not None:
350	                self.generators[req_index] = request.generator
351	
352	            if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
353	                self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
354	            if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
355	                self.num_prompt_logprobs[
356	                    req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
357	
358	            if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:
359	                self.has_allowed_token_ids.add(req_id)
360	                if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is None:
361	                    # Lazy allocation for this tensor, which can be large.
362	                    # False means we don't fill with -inf.
363	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask = torch.zeros(
364	                        self.max_num_reqs,
365	                        self.vocab_size,
366	                        dtype=torch.bool,
367	                        device=self.device)
368	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
369	                        self.max_num_reqs,
370	                        self.vocab_size,
371	                        dtype=torch.bool,
372	                        device="cpu")
373	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index] = True
374	                # False means we don't fill with -inf.
375	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index][
376	                    sampling_params.allowed_token_ids] = False
377	
378	            if sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids:
379	                self.bad_words_token_ids[
380	                    req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids
381	        else:
382	            assert request.pooling_params is not None
383	            self.pooling_params[req_id] = request.pooling_params
384	
385	        # Add request lora ID
386	        if request.lora_request:
387	            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id
388	            if lora_id not in self.lora_id_to_request_ids:
389	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id] = set()
390	
391	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = lora_id

400	    def remove_request(self, req_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
401	        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().
402	        
403	        Args:
404	          req_id: request to remove
405	
406	        Returns:
407	          Removed request index, or `None` if `req_id` not recognized
408	        """
409	
410	        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
411	        if req_index is None:
412	            return None
413	        self.batch_update_builder.removed_append(req_index)
414	        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
415	        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None
416	
417	        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
418	        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
419	        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
420	        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
421	        self.top_a_reqs.discard(req_id)
422	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.discard(req_id)
423	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
424	        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
425	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
426	        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
427	        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
428	        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
429	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)
430	
431	        # LoRA
432	        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
433	        if lora_id != 0:
434	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
435	            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
436	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
437	                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
438	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
439	
440	        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
441	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
442	            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
443	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
444	        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
445	        self.pooling_params.pop(req_id, None)
446	        return req_index

342	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
343	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
344	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
345	        the greedily-sampled token.
346	
347	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
348	        sampled token to 1.
349	
350	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
351	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
352	        """
353	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

356	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
357	    logits: torch.Tensor,
358	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
359	) -> torch.Tensor:
360	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
361	        have not been generated yet
362	    """
363	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
364	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
365	    logits_applied = 0
366	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
367	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
368	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
369	
370	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
371	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
372	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
373	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
374	            continue
375	
376	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
377	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
378	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
379	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
380	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
381	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
382	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
383	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
384	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
385	
386	            if seqs_to_penalize:
387	                # convert to the index into logits
388	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
389	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
390	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
391	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
392	
393	    if logits_to_penalize:
394	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
395	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
396	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
397	
398	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
399	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
400	    return logits
401	
402	
403	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
404	    logits: torch.Tensor,
405	    p: torch.Tensor,
406	    k: torch.Tensor,
407	) -> torch.Tensor:
408	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
409	
410	    # Apply top-k.
411	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
412	    # Get all the top_k values.
413	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
414	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
415	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
416	
417	    # Apply top-p.
418	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
419	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
420	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
421	    # at least one
422	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
423	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
424	
425	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
426	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
427	                                                    index=logits_idx,
428	                                                    src=logits_sort)
429	    return logits
430	
431	
432	def _apply_min_p(
433	    logits: torch.Tensor,
434	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
435	) -> torch.Tensor:
436	    """
437	    Adapted from
438	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
439	    """
440	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
441	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
442	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
443	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
444	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
445	
446	    return logits
447	
448	
449	def _apply_top_a(
450	    logits: torch.Tensor,
451	    top_a: torch.Tensor,
452	) -> torch.Tensor:
453	    """Apply Top-A sampling to logits.
454	
455	    Top-A keeps only tokens whose probability is at least
456	    top_a * max_probability, then masks the rest.
457	    """
458	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
459	    max_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
460	    threshold = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * max_probs
461	    tokens_to_remove = probs < threshold
462	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
463	
464	    return logits
465	
466	
467	def _greedy_sample(
468	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
469	    samples: torch.Tensor,
470	) -> SampleResultType:
471	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
472	
473	    Args:
474	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
475	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
476	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
477	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
478	    Returns:
479	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
480	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
481	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
482	    """
483	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
484	    sample_idx = 0
485	    results: SampleResultType = []
486	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
487	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
488	            results.append(([], []))
489	            continue
490	
491	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
492	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
493	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
494	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
495	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
496	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
497	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
498	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
499	    return results

500	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1]
501	        self.block_table.swap_row(i1, i2)

503	    def condense(self) -> None:
504	        """Slide non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
505	
506	        Any consecutive empty indices at the very end of the list are not
507	        filled.
508	
509	        Args:
510	          empty_req_indices: empty indices which may be filled.
511	
512	        Returns:
513	          swaps: list of (from,to) swap tuples for moved requests
514	          empty_req_indices: indices not filled by condensation
515	        """
516	        if not (empty_req_indices := self.batch_update_builder.removed):
517	            # All removed requests were replaced by added requests, or else no
518	            # requests were removed at all. No condense() needed
519	            return
520	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
521	        if num_reqs == 0:
522	            # The batched states are empty.
523	            self._req_ids.clear()
524	            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
525	            return
526	
527	        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
528	        # is sorted in descending order.
529	        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
530	        while empty_req_indices:
531	            # Find the largest non-empty index.
532	            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
533	                last_req_index -= 1
534	
535	            # Find the smallest empty index.
536	            empty_index = self.batch_update_builder.peek_removed()
537	            assert empty_index is not None
538	            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
539	                break
540	
541	            # Move active request down into empty request
542	            # index.
543	            self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()

734	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
735

432	def _apply_min_p(
433	    logits: torch.Tensor,
434	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
435	) -> torch.Tensor:
436	    """
437	    Adapted from
438	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
439	    """
440	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
441	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
442	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
443	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
444	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
445	
446	    return logits

449	def _apply_top_a(
450	    logits: torch.Tensor,
451	    top_a: torch.Tensor,
452	) -> torch.Tensor:
453	    """Apply Top-A sampling to logits.
454	
455	    Top-A keeps only tokens whose probability is at least
456	    top_a * max_probability, then masks the rest.
457	    """
458	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
459	    max_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
460	    threshold = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * max_probs
461	    tokens_to_remove = probs < threshold
462	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
463	
464	    return logits

298	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

content.text:
Confirmed: no `min_p` in V1 `SamplingMetadata`. The `top_a` was added alongside `top_p` and `top_k` which is correct for V1.

Here is my complete findings report:

---

## Review Summary

I reviewed all 6 files for the Top-A sampling implementation. The changes are largely correct and consistent. I found **3 issues**:

### Issue 1 (Bug): Return type annotation is wrong in `from_sampling_metadata`

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397619616-ox72ss/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, line 394

The return type annotation says:
```python
) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool, bool]:
```

But the function returns 5 values (`sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p, do_top_a`) -- that's the tensors object plus **4** bools, so the annotation needs **5** `bool` entries:
```python
) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool, bool, bool]:
```

The current annotation has only 4 `bool` entries, which was correct *before* `do_top_a` was added but was not updated.

### Issue 2 (Bug): `swap_states` is missing `top_a_cpu` swap

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397619616-ox72ss/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`, lines 448-501

The `swap_states` method swaps `top_p_cpu` (line 470-471) and `top_k_cpu` (line 472-473) between indices, but does **not** swap `top_a_cpu`. A line like the following is missing after the `top_k_cpu` swap:
```python
self.top_a_cpu[i1], self.top_a_cpu[i2] =\
    self.top_a_cpu[i2], self.top_a_cpu[i1]
```

Without this, swapping two requests in the batch will leave their `top_a` values in the wrong positions.

### Issue 3 (Bug): `condense` is missing `top_a_cpu` move

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397619616-ox72ss/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`, lines 556-596

The `condense` method copies `top_p_cpu` (line 569) and `top_k_cpu` (line 570) when moving a request from `last_req_index` to `empty_index`, but does **not** copy `top_a_cpu`. A line like the following is missing after the `top_k_cpu` copy:
```python
self.top_a_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_a_cpu[last_req_index]
```

Without this, condensing the batch (sliding requests down after removals) will leave stale `top_a` values.

### Everything else is correct

- **`vllm/sampling_params.py`**: Field declaration, docstring, `from_optional` passthrough, `__post_init__` greedy reset (`self.top_a = 0.0`), `_verify_args` validation (`0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0`), and `__repr__` all look correct and consistent with `min_p`/`top_p`/`top_k` patterns.
- **`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`**: `SamplingTensors` dataclass field, list construction in `from_sampling_metadata`, tensor creation in `from_lists`, and passing to the `cls()` constructor are all correct and consistent with other parameters. (Except the return type annotation issue noted above.)
- **`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`** (V0): `_init_sampling_tensors` correctly unpacks 5 values, `forward` correctly stores and uses `do_top_a`, `_apply_top_a` implementation is consistent with `_apply_min_p` (both use in-place `unsqueeze_`). Ordering (after top_k/top_p and min_p) is reasonable.
- **`vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`**: `top_a` field added alongside `top_p`/`top_k` as `Optional[torch.Tensor]`. Correct.
- **`vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`**: Buffer init, `add_request`, `remove_request`, `no_top_a` property, `_make_sampling_metadata` usage are all correct. (Except the `swap_states`/`condense` omissions noted above.)
- **`vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`** (V1): `_apply_top_a` implementation uses non-in-place `unsqueeze()` which is correct since it operates on a buffer slice. The `None` check before calling it is consistent with how `top_p`/`top_k` are handled.

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project

from array import array
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams, SamplingType
from vllm.sequence import (VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE, SequenceData,
                           SequenceGroupMetadata)
from vllm.utils import (PyObjectCache, async_tensor_h2d,
                        is_pin_memory_available, make_tensor_with_pad)

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5


@dataclass
class SequenceGroupToSample:
    # |---------- N-1 iteration --------|
    # |---------------- N iteration ---------------------|
    # |- tokenA -|......................|-- newTokens ---|
    # |---------- context_len ----------|
    # |-------------------- seq_len ----------------------|
    #                                   |-- query_len ---|

    # Sequence ids for the sequence group in a previous step.
    seq_ids: list[int]
    sampling_params: SamplingParams
    # seq_id -> sequence data.
    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
    # The length of the sequence (all tokens seen in the past + new token to
    # compute attention) of the sequence group. None if it is in a decode
    # stage.
    seq_len: Optional[int]
    # The length of new query tokens to compute in the current step. None if it
    # is in a decode stage. The length of query_len <= seq_len if chunked
    # prefill is enabled.
    query_len: Optional[int]
    # A random number generator for sampling.
    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
    # True if the sequence group is in prefill stage. False if it is in a
    # decode stage.
    is_prompt: bool
    # Query token indices from logits. to compute prompt logprob. Empty if
    # prompt logprob is not required.
    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
    # Sample token indices from logits. Empty if sampling is not required.
    sample_indices: list[int]

    @property
    def do_sample(self):
        return len(self.sample_indices) > 0

    def __post_init__(self):
        if len(self.prompt_logprob_indices) > 0:
            assert self.sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None
        if self.is_prompt:
            assert self.seq_len is not None
            assert self.query_len is not None


def gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs: int):
    return lambda: SequenceGroupToSample(
        seq_ids=[0] * num_seqs,
        sampling_params=None,
        seq_data=None,  # type: ignore
        seq_len=0,
        query_len=0,
        generator=None,
        is_prompt=True,
        prompt_logprob_indices=[],
        sample_indices=[],
    )


class SamplingMetadataCache:
    """Used to cache SamplingMetadata objects between scheduler iterations"""

    def __init__(self):
        self._seq_group_to_sample_cache: dict[int, PyObjectCache] = {}

    def get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(self, num_seqs):
        if num_seqs not in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache:
            self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs] = PyObjectCache(
                gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs))

        obj = self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs].get_object()
        return obj

    def reset(self):
        for cache in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache.values():
            cache.reset()


class SamplingMetadata:
    """Metadata for input sequences. Used in sampler.

    The usage is as follow;
    ```
    hidden_states = execute_model(...)
    logits = hidden_states[sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices]
    sample(logits)

    def sample(logits):
        # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling....
    ```

    Args:
        seq_groups: List of batched sequence groups.
        selected_token_indices: (num_query_tokens_to_logprob). Indices to find
            logits from the initial model output hidden states.
        categorized_sample_indices: SamplingType -> token indices to sample.
            Each token indices is 2D tensor of (num_indices, num_indices) where
            the first item means the sample index within the returned logit
            (before pruning padding), and the second item means the sample
            index after pruning using selected_token_indices.
            For example, if the returned logit is [1, 2, 3], and we select
            [1, 2] for sampling, the pruned logit will be [2, 3]. In this case,
            The first tuple is [1, 2] (sampled index within original logit),
            and the second tuple is [0, 1] (sampled index within pruned logit).
        num_prompts: Number of prompt sequence groups in seq_groups.
        skip_sampler_cpu_output: Indicates if we want to skip the GPU=>CPU
            serialization of token outputs.
        reuse_sampling_tensors: Indicates if we want to reuse sampling
            tensors that are part of the sampler forward pass. Currently,
            it is mainly used for multi-step decode.

    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
        num_prompts: int,
        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
        self.skip_sampler_cpu_output = skip_sampler_cpu_output
        self.reuse_sampling_tensors = reuse_sampling_tensors

    @staticmethod
    def prepare(
        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
        seq_lens: list[int],
        query_lens: list[int],
        device: str,
        pin_memory: bool,
        generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
        cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
    ) -> "SamplingMetadata":
        (
            seq_groups,
            selected_token_indices,
            categorized_sample_indices,
            num_prompts,
        ) = _prepare_seq_groups(seq_group_metadata_list, seq_lens, query_lens,
                                device, generators, cache)
        selected_token_indices = async_tensor_h2d(
            selected_token_indices,
            dtype=torch.long,
            target_device=device,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        categorized_sample_indices = {
            t:
            async_tensor_h2d(
                seq_ids,
                dtype=torch.int,
                target_device=device,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            for t, seq_ids in categorized_sample_indices.items()
        }

        sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata(
            seq_groups=seq_groups,
            selected_token_indices=selected_token_indices,
            categorized_sample_indices=categorized_sample_indices,
            num_prompts=num_prompts,
        )
        return sampling_metadata

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return (
            "SamplingMetadata("
            f"seq_groups={self.seq_groups}, "
            f"selected_token_indices={self.selected_token_indices}, "
            f"categorized_sample_indices={self.categorized_sample_indices})")


def _prepare_seq_groups(
    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
    seq_lens: list[int],
    query_lens: list[int],
    device: str,
    generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
    cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
) -> tuple[
        list[SequenceGroupToSample],
        list[int],
        dict[SamplingType, list[int]],
        int,
]:
    """Prepare sequence groups and indices for sampling.

    Args:
        seq_group_metadata_list: A list of sequence group to batch.
        seq_lens: A list of sequence lens per sequence group.
            Index of prompt len should match with seq_group_metadata_list.
        query_lens: A list of query lengths. Prompt lens include the length
            of entire prompt tokens, and it could be shorter.
        device: A device to use for random number generators,
            `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`.
        generators: A store of per-request random number generators used
            for seeded requests.

    Returns:
        seq_groups: A list of sequence group to sample.
        selected_token_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
        categorized_sample_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
        num_prompts: Total number of prompts from `seq_group_metadata_list`.
    """
    # Batched sequence groups for the current model forward stsep.
    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample] = []
    # A list of token indices to sample/compute logprob. It is used to
    # prune the outcome logits from the model for the performance.
    selected_token_indices: list[int] = []
    # Used for selected_token_indices.
    model_output_idx = 0

    # Sampling type -> (
    # indices to sample/prompt logprob within pruned output logits,
    # indices to sample within pruned logits)
    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
        t: []
        for t in SamplingType
    }
    # Index of logits to compute logprob. Logits include both prompt logprob
    # and sample logprob indices.
    logit_idx = 0
    # Total number of prompts from given sequence groups.
    num_prompts = 0

    for i, seq_group_metadata in enumerate(seq_group_metadata_list):
        seq_ids = seq_group_metadata.seq_data.keys()

        if cache is not None:
            sample_obj = cache.get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(len(seq_ids))

            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
                sample_obj.seq_ids[j] = seq_id

            sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices.clear()
            sample_obj.sample_indices.clear()

        sampling_params = seq_group_metadata.sampling_params
        is_prompt = seq_group_metadata.is_prompt
        generator: Optional[torch.Generator] = None
        # If the current seq group is in decode stage, it is None.
        seq_len: Optional[int] = None
        query_len: Optional[int] = None
        prompt_logprob_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices
                                             if cache is not None else [])
        sample_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.sample_indices
                                     if cache is not None else [])
        do_sample = seq_group_metadata.do_sample

        if seq_group_metadata.is_prompt:
            if sampling_params.seed is not None:
                generator = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(
                    sampling_params.seed)
                if generators is not None:
                    generators[seq_group_metadata.request_id] = generator

            num_prompts += 1
            num_prefill_sample = len(seq_ids)
            assert num_prefill_sample == 1
            assert query_lens is not None and seq_lens is not None
            query_len, seq_len = query_lens[i], seq_lens[i]
            # If we need sampling, exclude num_prefill_sample tokens from
            # prompt logprob.
            prompt_logprob_len = (query_len - num_prefill_sample
                                  if do_sample else query_len)
            sample_len = num_prefill_sample if do_sample else 0
        else:
            # Decode
            prompt_logprob_len = 0
            query_len = query_lens[i] if query_lens is not None and len(
                query_lens) > 0 else 1
            sample_len = len(seq_ids) * query_len if do_sample else 0

            if sampling_params.seed is not None and generators is not None:
                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)

        # Update indices to select from the model output.
        """
        This blocks computes selected_token_indices which is used in the
        following way.

        hidden_states = model(...)
        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
        """

        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
            selected_token_indices.extend(
                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
        model_output_idx += prompt_logprob_len
        if do_sample:
            selected_token_indices.extend(
                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + sample_len))
        model_output_idx += sample_len

        # We now find indices for logprob computation and sampling.
        """
        This block computes categorized_sample_indices which is used in the
        following way.

        hidden_states = model(...)
        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
        def sample(logits):
           # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling.
           # prompt_logprob_indices to find prompt logprob indices.
           # sample_indices to find sample indices.
        """

        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
            prompt_logprob_indices.extend(
                range(logit_idx, logit_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
            logit_idx += prompt_logprob_len
        if do_sample:
            sample_indices.extend(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len))
            categorized_sample_indices[sampling_params.sampling_type].extend(
                list(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len)))
            logit_idx += sample_len

        if cache is not None:
            sample_obj.sampling_params = sampling_params
            sample_obj.seq_data = seq_group_metadata.seq_data
            sample_obj.seq_len = seq_len
            sample_obj.query_len = query_len
            sample_obj.generator = generator
            sample_obj.is_prompt = is_prompt
        else:
            sample_obj = SequenceGroupToSample(
                seq_ids=list(seq_ids),
                sampling_params=sampling_params,
                seq_data=seq_group_metadata.seq_data,
                seq_len=seq_len,
                query_len=query_len,
                generator=generator,
                is_prompt=is_prompt,
                prompt_logprob_indices=list(prompt_logprob_indices),
                sample_indices=list(sample_indices),
            )

        seq_groups.append(sample_obj)

    if cache is not None:
        cache.reset()

    return (seq_groups, selected_token_indices, categorized_sample_indices,
            num_prompts)


@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_as: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        top_as: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False
        do_top_a = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
            top_a = sampling_params.top_a

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_top_a and top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_top_a = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                top_as += [top_a] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                top_as += [top_a] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            top_as,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p,
                do_top_a)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        top_as: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:
            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                prompt_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                output_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
        else:
            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            prompt_t = empty_tensor
            output_t = empty_tensor

        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
            temperatures,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            min_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_as_t = torch.tensor(
            top_as,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            presence_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            repetition_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ks,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
        # transfer to device.

        return cls(
            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_as=top_as_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                       non_blocking=True),
            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                         non_blocking=True),
            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                           non_blocking=True),
            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        )
